video_title: Top 10 #DDD Meatball Videos of All Time with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Pagoda Restaurant
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Fairbanks, Alaska, with the one and only Benny Lin from Pagoda Restaurant. Meatball subs in the oven? The meatball sub, it's really, really, really good. Their marinara sauce is just absolutely amazing. The bread, it's always soft. You definitely need two hands to pick it up and they are huge.

dishes.description: Homemade bread, ground pork and beef meatballs, housemade bread soaked in milk, onion, parsley, Parmesan, eggs, garlic, fennel, pepper, salt, homemade tomato sauce, provolone, and mozzarella, baked in the oven.
dishes.guy_response: That bread, with that little garlic kick, that's dynamite. Meatballs are tender, got great texture. Pasta's delicious. This really nice tomato, it's legit. I mean, I could easily be in an East Coast sub shop getting this. Good job. Thank you. The meatballs are spiced perfectly. Nice, zesty marinara sauce. It's killer. It is killer.

restaurants.name: Catelli's
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here at a cool little town called Geyserville in the Northern California wine country. That brings us here to Catelli's. Here we go, folks. Get your food ready. Where Nick Catelli and his sister, Dominica. Come home. But I grew up here. Kind of old school with a little bit of a modern twist. Yeah, like meatballs, sliders, and red sauce. They're amazing. It's a combination that really works.

dishes.description: Meatballs made with organic beef and house-made sausage, ricotta cheese, panko, brewer's yeast, garlic, onion, Parmesan, Italian parsley, thyme, salt, pepper, and egg, pan-fried and finished in the oven, served with homemade tomato sauce on buns.
dishes.guy_response: Delicious meatball. You can taste the ingredients. The richness of the beef melts in your mouth when you eat it. You know, when you cook something to order, the texture just, there's no replacing that.

restaurants.name: Rigoletto's Italian Bakery and Cafe
restaurants.guy_intro: Triple D's at Rigoletto's Italian Bakery and Cafe in Virginia Beach, Virginia. Is it big enough? It's huge. It is ridiculous. Mother Gilda and daughter Jessie are the bosses, the cooks, and definitely the heart of this place. Come in here, you may feel like you're one of the family. We're more like an Italian home, you know? People come in, we know their first name, we know what they drink, we know what they eat. Stuffed meatballs? Yes, sir. Dude, I'd weigh 5,000 pounds if I lived within 500 miles of this place.

dishes.description: Meatballs made with lean and fatty meats, eggs, breadcrumbs, dried parsley, Pecorino Romano, Parmigiano-Reggiano, garlic powder, salt, and pepper, stuffed with a mixture of general salami, spicy capicola, black horse ham, provolone, and mozzarella, baked low and slow, served with grandma's crushed tomato sauce and spaghetti.
dishes.guy_response: Look at that. Look at all that creaminess from the provolone and the matz. Because the inside is so creamy, you do need that hard exterior of that almost to keep it locked in. Yeah. Not overly flavored. You know, I was wondering with all those cold cuts in there, if there was going to be too much salt. A really nice. A little balance. A little sweetness coming out of that ham, a little out of the matz, a little salt from the provolone. Pasta is al dente. The sauce is sweet and just luscious and great tomato flavor.

restaurants.name: The Saucy Sicilian
restaurants.guy_intro: Gannon Mendez left the corporate world to open a food truck. This is the saucy Sicilian. He wanted the best help he could find, so he asked his mom, Priscilla. She was living in the mountains in Colorado, and I'm like, hey, I need you out in Oklahoma. Let's start a food truck. He got me out of retirement. O-M-G. You went. Old school Italian, just like my great grandmother. If you told somebody to come here, what should they have when they come here? Nona's meatballs. The meatballs are the must have? I would agree with that. Nona's sauce and meatballs.

dishes.description: Meatballs made with 50-50 pork and beef, eggs, garlic, homemade breadcrumbs, parsley, and Parmesan cheese, roasted in the oven, then simmered for eight hours in great grandma's marinara sauce with seared short rib, olive oil, garlic, onions, tomato paste, tomato sauce, sugar, salt, red pepper flake, black pepper, and bay leaves.
dishes.guy_response: Delicious and tender and moist. Great sauce. Put a little sprinkle of the parmesan right at the end. That fresh basil, hm, hits that sauce and just blooms. And that's a good amount of garlic.

restaurants.name: Astoria Cafe and Market
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Cleveland, Ohio. And it's been a while since I've visited. Let me tell you, my cousins remind me of it all the time. You know, the food scene is really blown up here. And with so much culture, when I hear that there's a Greek joint where they're importing their own goods, you know I got to check it out. This is Astoria Cafe and Market. Astoria is probably the most authentic Mediterranean restaurant in Cleveland. You can go all out. You can get a four-course meal. Or you can just come grab a beer and a pizza. It's a place to get fine imported wine and cheeses. Which is how cousins George Consios and Steve Daniels first rolled out, leaning into their Greek heritage in the import business. We started literally as a wholesale. And wanted to do primarily Mediterranean products. This is awesome. Beautiful market. Love the deli case. And when they expanded into a full-blown restaurant, they brought in chefs. Corey Korinski to keep the Mediterranean vibe alive.

dishes.dish_name: Veal, Lamb, and Ricotta Meatballs with Fettuccine
dishes.description: Meatballs made with ground lamb shoulder and veal chuck roll, mixed with Italian ricotta, parsley, minced garlic, kosher salt, black pepper, matsutake shoyu, milk, cream, and panko breadcrumbs, dredged in flour, fried in olive oil, and finished in the oven, served with house tomato sauce (olive oil, butter, red pepper flakes, Greek oregano, minced garlic, San Marzano tomatoes, carrots, yellow onions, basil stems, fish sauce) and house-made fettuccine pasta.
dishes.guy_response: That is without question one of the most tender, fluffiest, lightest flavored meatballs I've ever had. A little unique richness that comes in from the lamb. And the pasta is delicious, too. And the tomato sauce is spot on, very nice and rustic. That's what we call a destination meatball. That would go in the Flavortown Hall of Fame. That is light out.

restaurants.name: Folk Art
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Inman Park in Atlanta. Georgia, right between the Highlands and Little Five Points. Now to you and me, that doesn't really mean much. But to the locals, this is a famous neighborhood. They're just blowing up with some righteous restaurants like this joint. Welcome to Folk Art. This place is the definition of southern hospitality. The menu's great from top to bottom. And cooked up by Atlanta native Jason Hill. Who with co-owner Randy Parks turned a 100-year-old former grocery store into this? A funky folksy joint in 2013. What's the food? Keep it simple, but we dress it up. Make it artful. Sort of like a funky upscale. Funky upscale.

dishes.description: Burger patties made with ground croutons, raw diced onions, raw fresh garlic, cumin, black pepper, fresh thyme, oregano, chili powder, salt, and fresh eggs, cooked on a flat top, topped with provolone cheese, caramelized onions, mushrooms, house-made marinara sauce, and house-made pickles, served on a bun with hand-cut fries.
dishes.guy_response: Love the sauteed mushrooms. Nice little kiss of the marinara. That right there screaming Italian family Sunday dinner, that's ridiculously big. The sandwiches are like complete meals. It's got some southern kick to it, and it makes it totally awesome.
